name: Foz de Arbayún Loop from Lumbier
name_original: Reserva Natural de la Foz de Arbayun desde Lumbier
description: This moderate 17.7-mile road cycling route from Lumbier offers 1442 feet of elevation gain and dramatic views of the Foz de Arbayún Natural
region_intro: Road cycling around Lumbier, located in the Navarra region of Spain, offers diverse terrain for cyclists. The area features dramatic gorges, river valleys, and limestone cliffs, providing varied landscapes. Cyclists encounter both accessible greenways and routes with significant elevation gains through varied forest masses. The region's natural features contribute to a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Lumbier The most popular road cycling route is Return to Peña Izaga from Lumbier, a 31.6 miles (50.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 21 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge with notable elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Foz de Arbayún Loop from Lumbier, a moderate 17.7 miles (28.4 km) path. This route provides views of the Foz de Arbayún Natural Park. Local road cyclists also love the Alzorriz Fountain – Ascent to Alzorriz loop from Lumbier, a 38.1 miles (61.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 0 minutes. Road cycling around Lumbier is defined by dramatic gorges, river valleys, and limestone cliffs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from accessible greenways to routes with significant elevation. The routes in Lumbier are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Lumbier's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Lumbier?
A: The Lumbier area offers a diverse road cycling experience, characterized by dramatic gorges, river valleys, and impressive limestone cliffs. You'll find routes ranging from accessible greenways, like sections of the historic Irati Greenway, to more challenging paths with significant elevation gains as you venture towards the Navarrese Pyrenees. The varied forest masses also contribute to a constantly changing and engaging cycling environment.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Lumbier region?
A: There are 15 road cycling routes available around Lumbier on komoot. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with 10 moderate and 5 difficult options to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or easy road cycling options in Lumbier?
A: While the komoot guide primarily features moderate to difficult road cycling routes, the region does offer easier options. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5087670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irati Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, which follows sections of the former Irati railway line, provides a flat and accessible path. It passes directly through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2933330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Lumbier&lt;/a&gt;, making it ideal for touring cyclists and families looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints to see while cycling in Lumbier?
A: Road cycling routes in Lumbier often feature stunning natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2933330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Lumbier&lt;/a&gt; is a spectacular canyon with impressive limestone cliffs, which you can traverse via a path following the old Irati railway line, including two ancient train tunnels. Further afield, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4099433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Arbayun&lt;/a&gt; offers even larger gorges and is a critical habitat for griffon vultures, often visible from viewpoints. Many routes also provide views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5187583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Itzaga Peak&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Lumbier?
A: Lumbier offers appealing cycling conditions for much of the year. The region's climate generally allows for pleasant riding in spring, summer, and autumn. During these seasons, you can best enjoy the scenic routes and natural features without extreme weather. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by or offer views of historical and cultural landmarks. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31901951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Xavier – Javier Castle loop from Lumbier&lt;/a&gt; takes you near the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/934414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Xavier&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the town of Lumbier itself has Roman origins and features a historical quarter with the 14th-century Church of the Assumption.

Q: Where can I park when visiting the Foz de Lumbier for cycling?
A: Parking is available near the Foz de Lumbier, making it convenient to access the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5087670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Irati Greenway&lt;/a&gt; and the gorge itself. Specific parking areas are usually signposted as you approach the natural reserve.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I spot while road cycling in the Lumbier gorges?
A: The gorges around Lumbier, particularly the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2933330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Lumbier&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4099433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Arbayun&lt;/a&gt;, are renowned for their birdlife. You have a high chance of spotting griffon vultures, peregrine falcons, and other raptors that nest in the towering limestone cliffs. These areas are protected nature reserves, making them prime locations for birdwatching.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Lumbier?
A: The road cycling routes in Lumbier are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the dramatic scenery, the unique experience of riding through the old train tunnels in the Foz de Lumbier, and the diverse terrain that caters to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs near Lumbier?
A: Yes, for cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e985109161/return-to-pena-izaga-from-lumbier" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to Peña Izaga from Lumbier&lt;/a&gt; offer notable elevation gains, reaching over 630 meters. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10524559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alzorriz Fountain – Ascent to Alzorriz loop from Lumbier&lt;/a&gt;, features an even more significant climb of over 860 meters, providing a rewarding experience with dramatic views.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes starting from Lumbier?
A: Many of the road cycling routes around Lumbier are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986660651/foz-de-arbayun-loop-from-lumbier" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Arbayún Loop from Lumbier&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20519890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views towards Cáseda loop from Lumbier&lt;/a&gt;, making it convenient for day trips.
tips: A true spectacle of nature. There is no merit in reaching it, but it is worth leaning over the precipice from the safe fence of the viewpoint.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for road cycling, considering the Foz de Arbayún's rugged terrain?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is designed for racebikes and approaches the Natural Reserve of the Foz de Arbayún from Lumbier, it's important to note that direct road cycling *within* the gorge itself is generally not recommended. The internal trails of the gorge are rugged, with steep, rocky, and sometimes cobbled sections, primarily suited for hiking or mountain biking. This route offers a road cycling approach to experience the area's periphery and viewpoints.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This racebike route primarily follows paved roads and well-maintained tracks leading towards the Natural Reserve. You can expect a moderate difficulty with some elevation gain, as indicated by the 439 meters of ascent. The route includes sections like Avenida Salazar, Calle Iso, and Camino de Arbonés, which are generally suitable for road bikes.

faqs.question: What are the best viewpoints or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight of this route is the Foz de Arbayun itself, a spectacular gorge. While the route doesn't go deep into the gorge, it leads to areas where you can appreciate its grandeur. Look for designated viewpoints, such as the Iso lookout, which offers panoramic views of the canyon and its impressive vertical walls.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see in the Foz de Arbayún Natural Reserve area?
faqs.answer: The Foz de Arbayún is renowned as a sanctuary for various wildlife, especially birds of prey. You have an excellent chance of spotting one of Spain's largest colonies of griffon vultures soaring above the cliffs, along with peregrine falcons and golden eagles. The diverse flora includes beech, ash, and holm oak.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle this route. The weather is usually milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially slippery conditions on some sections, especially after rain.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this route and in the Natural Reserve?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally permitted in many natural areas, specific regulations for the Foz de Arbayún Natural Reserve may apply, especially regarding wildlife protection. It's advisable to keep dogs on a leash at all times to protect the local fauna, particularly the ground-nesting birds and other wildlife. Always check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Natural Reserve of the Foz de Arbayún?
faqs.answer: The Natural Reserve of the Foz de Arbayún generally does not require an entrance fee or specific permits for access to its main viewpoints or the approach roads. However, if you plan to venture onto specific internal hiking trails, it's always good practice to check the official Visit Navarra website for any updated regulations or special requirements, especially for guided activities or larger groups.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this cycling route from Lumbier?
faqs.answer: Lumbier, being the starting point, offers various parking options. Look for public parking areas within the town. Specific parking for the Foz de Arbayún is often available at designated viewpoints or trailheads leading into the reserve, but for this racebike route starting from Lumbier, parking within the town itself would be most convenient.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of local roads and paths. You will cycle along parts of Avenida Salazar, Calle Iso, and Camino de Arbonés, which are common thoroughfares in the area. These are generally local roads or tracks, not necessarily dedicated long-distance trails.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the route?
faqs.answer: Lumbier, as a nearby town, offers amenities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. It's a good place to refuel or find lodging before or after your ride. Along the route itself, especially closer to the Natural Reserve, services may be limited, so plan accordingly.
